content_of_post_to_answer: Psychology of Everyday Life: What do people fear most?
answer: People rarely fear specific outcomes as much as they fear being interpreted wrongly—by others, and eventually by themselves. Most anxieties about failure, rejection, or uncertainty collapse into this: loss of control over the story others form about you. That’s why the same event can feel tolerable or unbearable depending on who might witness it. I've written more on this exact pattern on my profile, if you want to go deeper.
